description_long: This sculpture depicts a crowned female figure, possibly the Virgin Mary, seated and holding the lifeless body of a man, likely Jesus Christ, across her lap in a classic Pietà composition. The woman is adorned in flowing robes and a crown, with a veil draping over her shoulders and back. The man’s body is limp, with detailed anatomical features and a sorrowful expression. The sculpture is mounted on an ornate pedestal featuring a carved face or mask at the center. The back of the sculpture is less detailed, with the drapery of the robe forming the main visible feature. The entire piece appears to be carved from weathered stone, with a patina suggesting age and exposure to the elements.
description: A weathered stone sculpture of a crowned woman holding a man's body across her lap, set atop an ornate pedestal. The figures are detailed in front, with flowing robes and expressive faces.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing the crowned woman seated with the man's body draped across her lap, both figures' faces and details clearly visible.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the profile of the woman and the length of the man's body, with the crown and drapery visible.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the back of the woman's robe and crown, with minimal detail and the pedestal's back visible.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the woman's profile, the man's body, and the draped robes, with the pedestal's side visible.
attribute_subject_matter: Pietà with crowned female figure and male body
attribute_location_context: No visible context; isolated on a white background.
